description: Two cards down, the chain wanted a third. Topple the Statue (Act 2) and Despark (Act 3 main) were already mirrored by a prior pass. Prison Realm — the Act 3 coda, Bolas contained for eternity under Ugin's expansion of the renamed Meditation Plane — sat unconnected to either, despite being the canonical bookend of the same Bolas-Falls story-spotlight cluster the existing edge names. Both endpoints carry Scryfall's story_spotlight: true. Both flavor texts name Bolas directly. The narrative ordering is Wizards-stated. A mirror was the obvious play. Sigh applies. The shape is now a chain (Topple → Despark → Prison Realm); a future pass to draw Topple ↔ Prison Realm completes the triangle.
citations: [MTG Wiki: War of the Spark — Story Spotlights]
[MTG Wiki: Meditation Plane — renamed Prison Realm post-WAR, eternal containment under Ugin]

other_candidates_considered.reason_deferred: Adding the same pass would have completed the Topple-Despark-Prison-Realm triangle in one invocation. Spec is one edge per invocation. This is the next pass — draws the third leg, mood shifts to 'satisfied' on the triangle reward.

Two of five Triumph cycle members enriched in corpus (Gideon WAR-015, Liliana WAR-098, Chandra WAR-121 absent). Same templated flavor construction ('X's triumph came not from Y, but from Z'), same artist (Kieran Yanner), same Hour of Devastation 'Defeat' cycle bookend. Strong category-clean mirror but a less elegant topology than the chain completion above. Worth a follow-up pass once a third Triumph card lands — that would trigger Mr. Nodeley with three cards on a designer-confirmed-cycle, paralleling the fear-of-cycle precedent.

other_candidates_considered.reason_refused: Only two corpus cards have the Survival mechanic (Cautious Survivor DSK-172, Glimmer Seeker DSK-14). E1 already refused Glimmer cycle on count grounds. Same count, same refusal. Re-surveys when the corpus accumulates 3+ Survival-mechanic cards.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Aetherdrift second-faction character node beyond Endriders
other_candidates_considered.reason_deferred: Aether Rangers already exists as the Avishkar home-team node (commissioned in a prior pass). The next-strongest Aetherdrift faction node would require a corpus survey for canonical-faction-membership citations across the remaining eight Grand Prix teams (Gloryguard, Iron Squire, Smiling Slitherlords, etc.) and three or more enriched corpus members per faction. Out of scope for a single-edge invocation; flag as a future bbl-edgelord run with the Aetherdrift set as the candidate-scope.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Azorius signet symbol node (parallel to Orzhov Signet)
other_candidates_considered.reason_deferred: Caveat on the Azorius Senate character node already names this as the obvious future split (three-pointed angular Azorius sigil on Sphinx of New Prahv chest emblem, Azorius Locket centerpiece, New Prahv architectural ornamentation). The three corpus cards plausibly carrying the sigil are not yet vision-survey-confirmed at iconographic resolution — same parity barrier as Orzhov Signet had before the symbols layer survey. Wait for an iconographic vision-pass to confirm sigil-on-art across at least two corpus cards before Mr. Nodeley triggers.
